---
title: "MP094: Platform API Architecture"
subtitle: "Establishes the standardized organization, naming conventions, and implementation patterns for platform APIs, ensuring consistent integration of diverse data sources through a unified interface architecture"
chapter: "CH00"
category: "meta-principle"
number: "MP094"
date-created: "2025-08-27"
date-modified: "2025-08-27"
author: "Claude"
type: "meta-principle"
law: "Fundamental Law"
article-number: 
  number: 94
  title: "Platform API Architecture"
related_to:
  - "MP092: Platform ID Standard"
  - "MP065: Platform Configuration Management"
  - "MP064: ETL Derivation Separation"
  - "SO_R001: Directory Structure"
  - "DM_R022: Platform Numbering Convention"
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    code-fold: false
    code-tools: true
    number-sections: true
---

# Article 94: Platform API Architecture {#overview}

**Fundamental Law Article 94 (Platform API Architecture)**

This meta-principle establishes the canonical architecture for organizing, implementing, and managing platform APIs across the system, ensuring consistent access patterns for diverse data sources including APIs, CSV exports, databases, and manual data entry.

:::{.callout-critical}
## Absolute Requirement
All platform integrations MUST follow the standardized API architecture defined herein. Platform-specific code must be organized in designated directories using three-letter platform_ids, with consistent naming conventions and configuration patterns. This ensures maintainability, reusability, and systematic integration across all data sources.
:::

# Section 1: Directory Organization {#directory-organization}

## Subsection 1: Platform API Structure {#api-structure}

Platform APIs shall be organized in a dedicated directory within global_scripts:

```
global_scripts/
├── 26_platform_apis/              # Platform API implementations
│   ├── README.md                  # Architecture documentation
│   ├── api_registry.yaml          # Registry of all platform APIs
│   ├── common/                    # Shared API utilities
│   │   ├── fn_api_rate_limiter.R
│   │   ├── fn_api_error_handler.R
│   │   ├── fn_api_authenticator.R
│   │   ├── fn_api_cache_manager.R
│   │   └── fn_api_response_parser.R
│   ├── cbz/                       # Cyberbiz platform
│   │   ├── api_config.yaml       # Platform-specific configuration
│   │   ├── fn_cbz_connect.R      # Connection management
│   │   ├── fn_cbz_authenticate.R # Authentication
│   │   ├── fn_cbz_get_customers.R
│   │   ├── fn_cbz_get_orders.R
│   │   ├── fn_cbz_get_products.R
│   │   ├── fn_cbz_get_inventory.R
│   │   └── tests/                # Platform-specific tests
│   ├── eby/                       # eBay platform
│   │   ├── api_config.yaml
│   │   ├── fn_eby_connect.R
│   │   ├── fn_eby_authenticate.R
│   │   ├── fn_eby_get_listings.R
│   │   ├── fn_eby_get_orders.R
│   │   └── fn_eby_get_analytics.R
│   ├── amz/                       # Amazon platform
│   │   ├── api_config.yaml
│   │   ├── fn_amz_connect.R
│   │   └── fn_amz_get_sales.R
│   ├── web/                       # Official website
│   │   ├── api_config.yaml
│   │   └── fn_web_connect_db.R
│   └── csv/                       # CSV import handlers
│       ├── api_config.yaml
│       ├── fn_csv_import_sales.R
│       └── fn_csv_validate_format.R
```

## Subsection 2: Configuration File Structure {#config-structure}

Each platform must have an `api_config.yaml` file:

```yaml
# api_config.yaml template
platform:
  platform_id: "cbz"  # Three-letter platform_id
  name: "Cyberbiz"
  version: "2.0"
  
api:
  base_url: "https://api.cyberbiz.com/v2"
  auth_type: "api_key"  # oauth2, api_key, basic, signature
  
  endpoints:
    customers:
      path: "/customers"
      method: "GET"
      rate_limit: 100  # requests per minute
      cache_ttl: 3600  # seconds
      
    orders:
      path: "/orders"
      method: "GET"
      rate_limit: 60
      pagination:
        type: "offset"  # offset, cursor, page
        limit: 100
        
    products:
      path: "/products"
      method: "GET"
      rate_limit: 120
      
authentication:
  api_key:
    header: "X-API-Key"
    env_var: "CYBERBIZ_API_KEY"
    
  oauth2:
    auth_url: "https://auth.cyberbiz.com/oauth/authorize"
    token_url: "https://auth.cyberbiz.com/oauth/token"
    scope: "read:all"
    
rate_limiting:
  strategy: "token_bucket"  # token_bucket, sliding_window
  max_requests: 5000
  per_period: 3600  # seconds
  retry_after: true
  
error_handling:
  retry_count: 3
  backoff_strategy: "exponential"  # linear, exponential
  timeout: 30  # seconds
  
data_mapping:
  customers:
    id: "customer_id"
    email: "email_address"
    name: "full_name"
    
  orders:
    id: "order_number"
    date: "order_date"
    total: "total_amount"
```

# Section 2: Naming Conventions {#naming-conventions}

## Subsection 1: Function Naming Pattern {#function-naming}

All platform API functions must follow the pattern:

```
fn_{platform_id}_{action}_{object}.R
```

Examples:
```r
# Connection and authentication
fn_cbz_connect.R           # Establish connection
fn_cbz_authenticate.R       # Handle authentication
fn_cbz_disconnect.R         # Clean disconnect

# Data retrieval
fn_cbz_get_customers.R     # Get customer data
fn_cbz_get_orders.R         # Get order data
fn_cbz_get_products.R       # Get product catalog

# Data manipulation
fn_cbz_update_inventory.R  # Update inventory levels
fn_cbz_create_order.R       # Create new order
fn_cbz_delete_product.R     # Remove product

# Specialized operations
fn_cbz_sync_catalog.R       # Synchronize product catalog
fn_cbz_calculate_metrics.R  # Platform-specific calculations
```

## Subsection 2: Common Function Interface {#common-interface}

All data retrieval functions must implement a consistent interface:

```r
fn_{platform}_get_{object} <- function(
  connection = NULL,
  filters = list(),
  fields = NULL,
  limit = NULL,
  offset = NULL,
  config = NULL,
  cache = TRUE,
  verbose = FALSE
) {
  # Standard implementation pattern
  
  # 1. Validate connection
  if (is.null(connection)) {
    connection <- fn_{platform}_connect(config = config)
    on.exit(fn_{platform}_disconnect(connection))
  }
  
  # 2. Build request
  request <- build_request(
    endpoint = get_endpoint("{object}"),
    filters = filters,
    fields = fields,
    limit = limit,
    offset = offset
  )
  
  # 3. Check cache
  if (cache) {
    cached <- check_cache(request)
    if (!is.null(cached)) return(cached)
  }
  
  # 4. Apply rate limiting
  wait_for_rate_limit(connection$rate_limiter)
  
  # 5. Execute request with error handling
  response <- tryCatch({
    execute_request(connection, request)
  }, error = function(e) {
    handle_api_error(e, connection, request)
  })
  
  # 6. Parse response
  data <- parse_response(response, object = "{object}")
  
  # 7. Cache if enabled
  if (cache) {
    cache_response(request, data)
  }
  
  # 8. Return standardized format
  return(data)
}
```

# Section 3: Unified Data Access Layer {#unified-access}

## Subsection 1: Platform Abstraction {#platform-abstraction}

Create a unified interface for all platforms:

```r
# fn_platform_get_data.R
fn_platform_get_data <- function(
  platform_id,
  data_type,
  filters = list(),
  config = NULL
) {
  # Validate platform_id
  validate_platform_id(platform_id)
  
  # Load platform-specific function
  fn_name <- paste0("fn_", platform_id, "_get_", data_type)
  
  if (!exists(fn_name)) {
    stop(paste("Function", fn_name, "not found for platform", platform_id))
  }
  
  # Call platform-specific function
  fn <- get(fn_name)
  return(fn(filters = filters, config = config))
}

# Usage examples
customers_cbz <- fn_platform_get_data("cbz", "customers")
orders_eby <- fn_platform_get_data("eby", "orders")
products_all <- fn_platform_get_data("all", "products")
```

## Subsection 2: Multi-Source Integration {#multi-source}

Support different data source types per platform:

```r
fn_get_platform_data_source <- function(platform_id) {
  config <- load_platform_config(platform_id)
  
  data_source <- switch(
    config$data$source_type,
    "api" = {
      list(
        type = "api",
        connect = get(paste0("fn_", platform_id, "_connect")),
        get = get(paste0("fn_", platform_id, "_get"))
      )
    },
    "csv" = {
      list(
        type = "csv",
        import = fn_csv_import,
        validate = fn_csv_validate
      )
    },
    "database" = {
      list(
        type = "database",
        connect = fn_db_connect,
        query = fn_db_query
      )
    },
    "manual" = {
      list(
        type = "manual",
        form = fn_manual_entry_form,
        validate = fn_manual_validate
      )
    },
    stop("Unknown data source type: ", config$data$source_type)
  )
  
  return(data_source)
}
```

# Section 4: Implementation Standards {#implementation-standards}

## Subsection 1: Authentication Management {#authentication}

Each platform must implement secure authentication:

```r
# fn_{platform}_authenticate.R template
fn_cbz_authenticate <- function(config = NULL) {
  if (is.null(config)) {
    config <- yaml::read_yaml("api_config.yaml")
  }
  
  auth_type <- config$api$auth_type
  
  credentials <- switch(
    auth_type,
    "api_key" = {
      key <- Sys.getenv(config$authentication$api_key$env_var)
      if (key == "") {
        stop("API key not found in environment variable: ", 
             config$authentication$api_key$env_var)
      }
      list(
        type = "api_key",
        header = config$authentication$api_key$header,
        value = key
      )
    },
    "oauth2" = {
      fn_oauth2_authenticate(
        auth_url = config$authentication$oauth2$auth_url,
        token_url = config$authentication$oauth2$token_url,
        scope = config$authentication$oauth2$scope
      )
    },
    "basic" = {
      fn_basic_authenticate(
        username = Sys.getenv(paste0(toupper(platform_id), "_USERNAME")),
        password = Sys.getenv(paste0(toupper(platform_id), "_PASSWORD"))
      )
    },
    stop("Unsupported authentication type: ", auth_type)
  )
  
  return(credentials)
}
```

## Subsection 2: Rate Limiting {#rate-limiting}

Implement rate limiting for API calls:

```r
# common/fn_api_rate_limiter.R
fn_create_rate_limiter <- function(config) {
  limiter <- list(
    max_requests = config$rate_limiting$max_requests,
    per_period = config$rate_limiting$per_period,
    requests = list(),
    strategy = config$rate_limiting$strategy
  )
  
  class(limiter) <- "rate_limiter"
  return(limiter)
}

fn_check_rate_limit <- function(limiter) {
  current_time <- Sys.time()
  
  # Remove old requests outside the window
  cutoff_time <- current_time - limiter$per_period
  limiter$requests <- limiter$requests[
    sapply(limiter$requests, function(x) x > cutoff_time)
  ]
  
  # Check if limit exceeded
  if (length(limiter$requests) >= limiter$max_requests) {
    wait_time <- as.numeric(
      min(limiter$requests) + limiter$per_period - current_time,
      units = "secs"
    )
    
    if (wait_time > 0) {
      message("Rate limit reached. Waiting ", round(wait_time, 1), " seconds...")
      Sys.sleep(wait_time)
    }
  }
  
  # Record new request
  limiter$requests <- c(limiter$requests, list(current_time))
  
  return(limiter)
}
```

## Subsection 3: Error Handling {#error-handling}

Standardized error handling across platforms:

```r
# common/fn_api_error_handler.R
fn_handle_api_error <- function(error, connection, request, config = NULL) {
  error_class <- class(error)[1]
  
  # Log error
  log_api_error(
    platform = connection$platform_id,
    endpoint = request$endpoint,
    error = error,
    timestamp = Sys.time()
  )
  
  # Determine if retry is appropriate
  should_retry <- error_class %in% c(
    "http_500", "http_502", "http_503", "http_504",
    "timeout_error", "connection_error"
  )
  
  if (should_retry && request$retry_count > 0) {
    # Exponential backoff
    wait_time <- 2^(config$error_handling$retry_count - request$retry_count)
    message("Retrying after ", wait_time, " seconds...")
    Sys.sleep(wait_time)
    
    # Retry with decremented count
    request$retry_count <- request$retry_count - 1
    return(execute_request(connection, request))
  }
  
  # Transform error for user
  user_error <- switch(
    error_class,
    "http_401" = "Authentication failed. Please check credentials.",
    "http_403" = "Access forbidden. Insufficient permissions.",
    "http_404" = "Resource not found.",
    "http_429" = "Rate limit exceeded. Please try again later.",
    paste("API error:", error$message)
  )
  
  stop(user_error, call. = FALSE)
}
```

# Section 5: Migration Path {#migration}

## Subsection 1: From Archive to Production {#archive-migration}

Steps to migrate existing API code:

```r
# Migration helper script
fn_migrate_api_from_archive <- function(
  archive_path,
  platform_id,
  target_dir = "scripts/global_scripts/26_platform_apis"
) {
  # 1. Create platform directory
  platform_dir <- file.path(target_dir, platform_id)
  if (!dir.exists(platform_dir)) {
    dir.create(platform_dir, recursive = TRUE)
  }
  
  # 2. Identify API functions in archive
  archive_files <- list.files(
    archive_path, 
    pattern = "\\.R$", 
    recursive = TRUE,
    full.names = TRUE
  )
  
  # 3. Rename and organize functions
  for (file in archive_files) {
    content <- readLines(file)
    
    # Extract function name
    fn_pattern <- "^\\s*([a-zA-Z_][a-zA-Z0-9_.]*)\\s*<-\\s*function"
    fn_matches <- grep(fn_pattern, content, value = TRUE)
    
    if (length(fn_matches) > 0) {
      old_name <- sub(fn_pattern, "\\1", fn_matches[1])
      
      # Determine new name based on function purpose
      new_name <- determine_new_function_name(old_name, platform_id)
      
      # Update function name in content
      content <- gsub(old_name, new_name, content)
      
      # Write to new location
      new_file <- file.path(platform_dir, paste0(new_name, ".R"))
      writeLines(content, new_file)
      
      message("Migrated: ", basename(file), " -> ", basename(new_file))
    }
  }
  
  # 4. Generate api_config.yaml
  generate_platform_config(platform_id, platform_dir)
  
  # 5. Update references in ETL scripts
  update_etl_references(platform_id)
  
  message("Migration complete for platform: ", platform_id)
}
```

## Subsection 2: ETL Script Updates {#etl-updates}

Update ETL scripts to use new API structure:

```r
# Before migration (in update_scripts/cbz_ETL01_0IM.R)
source("archive/MAMBA/cyberbiz_api/api_functions.R")
data <- get_cyberbiz_data()

# After migration
source("scripts/global_scripts/26_platform_apis/cbz/fn_cbz_connect.R")
source("scripts/global_scripts/26_platform_apis/cbz/fn_cbz_get_orders.R")

connection <- fn_cbz_connect()
data <- fn_cbz_get_orders(connection = connection)
fn_cbz_disconnect(connection)
```

# Section 6: Testing Requirements {#testing}

## Subsection 1: Platform API Tests {#api-tests}

Each platform must have comprehensive tests:

```r
# cbz/tests/test_cbz_api.R
test_that("Cyberbiz connection works", {
  config <- load_test_config()
  connection <- fn_cbz_connect(config = config)
  
  expect_true(is.list(connection))
  expect_equal(connection$platform_id, "cbz")
  expect_true(connection$authenticated)
  
  fn_cbz_disconnect(connection)
})

test_that("Cyberbiz customer retrieval works", {
  mock_connection <- create_mock_connection("cbz")
  customers <- fn_cbz_get_customers(
    connection = mock_connection,
    limit = 10
  )
  
  expect_true(is.data.frame(customers))
  expect_true("customer_id" %in% names(customers))
  expect_true(nrow(customers) <= 10)
})

test_that("Rate limiting is enforced", {
  limiter <- fn_create_rate_limiter(
    list(
      rate_limiting = list(
        max_requests = 2,
        per_period = 1
      )
    )
  )
  
  # First two requests should pass quickly
  start_time <- Sys.time()
  limiter <- fn_check_rate_limit(limiter)
  limiter <- fn_check_rate_limit(limiter)
  
  # Third request should wait
  limiter <- fn_check_rate_limit(limiter)
  elapsed <- as.numeric(Sys.time() - start_time, units = "secs")
  
  expect_true(elapsed >= 1)
})
```

## Subsection 2: Integration Tests {#integration-tests}

Test cross-platform functionality:

```r
test_that("Multi-platform data aggregation works", {
  # Get data from multiple platforms
  data_cbz <- fn_platform_get_data("cbz", "orders")
  data_eby <- fn_platform_get_data("eby", "orders")
  
  # Combine using standard format
  all_orders <- bind_rows(
    data_cbz %>% mutate(platform_id = "cbz"),
    data_eby %>% mutate(platform_id = "eby")
  )
  
  expect_true("platform_id" %in% names(all_orders))
  expect_true(all(all_orders$platform_id %in% c("cbz", "eby")))
})
```

# Section 7: Documentation Requirements {#documentation}

## Subsection 1: API Registry {#api-registry}

Maintain a central registry of all platform APIs:

```yaml
# api_registry.yaml
platforms:
  cbz:
    name: "Cyberbiz"
    status: "active"
    data_sources: ["api", "csv"]
    implemented_functions:
      - fn_cbz_connect
      - fn_cbz_get_customers
      - fn_cbz_get_orders
      - fn_cbz_get_products
    last_updated: "2025-08-27"
    maintainer: "platform_team"
    
  eby:
    name: "eBay"
    status: "active"
    data_sources: ["api"]
    implemented_functions:
      - fn_eby_connect
      - fn_eby_get_listings
      - fn_eby_get_orders
    last_updated: "2025-08-27"
    
  amz:
    name: "Amazon"
    status: "planned"
    data_sources: ["api", "csv"]
    implemented_functions: []
    notes: "Awaiting MWS to SP-API migration"
```

## Subsection 2: Function Documentation {#function-docs}

Each function must be documented:

```r
#' Get Customer Data from Cyberbiz
#'
#' Retrieves customer information from the Cyberbiz platform API.
#'
#' @param connection API connection object from fn_cbz_connect()
#' @param filters list of filters to apply (e.g., list(created_after = "2024-01-01"))
#' @param fields character vector of fields to retrieve (NULL for all)
#' @param limit maximum number of records to retrieve
#' @param offset number of records to skip
#' @param config optional configuration override
#' @param cache logical, whether to use cached results
#' @param verbose logical, whether to print progress messages
#'
#' @return data.frame with customer information
#' 
#' @examples
#' \dontrun{
#' conn <- fn_cbz_connect()
#' customers <- fn_cbz_get_customers(
#'   connection = conn,
#'   filters = list(created_after = "2024-01-01"),
#'   limit = 100
#' )
#' fn_cbz_disconnect(conn)
#' }
#'
#' @export
fn_cbz_get_customers <- function(
  connection = NULL,
  filters = list(),
  fields = NULL,
  limit = NULL,
  offset = NULL,
  config = NULL,
  cache = TRUE,
  verbose = FALSE
) {
  # Implementation
}
```

# Section 8: Compliance and Enforcement {#compliance}

## Subsection 1: Validation Rules {#validation-rules}

All platform API implementations must pass validation:

```r
fn_validate_platform_api <- function(platform_id) {
  platform_dir <- file.path(
    "scripts/global_scripts/26_platform_apis",
    platform_id
  )
  
  # Check directory structure
  expect_true(dir.exists(platform_dir))
  expect_true(file.exists(file.path(platform_dir, "api_config.yaml")))
  
  # Check naming conventions
  api_files <- list.files(platform_dir, pattern = "^fn_.*\\.R$")
  for (file in api_files) {
    expect_true(
      grepl(paste0("^fn_", platform_id, "_"), file),
      info = paste("File", file, "doesn't follow naming convention")
    )
  }
  
  # Check required functions
  required_functions <- c("connect", "disconnect", "authenticate")
  for (fn in required_functions) {
    fn_file <- paste0("fn_", platform_id, "_", fn, ".R")
    expect_true(
      fn_file %in% api_files,
      info = paste("Missing required function:", fn_file)
    )
  }
  
  # Check configuration validity
  config <- yaml::read_yaml(file.path(platform_dir, "api_config.yaml"))
  expect_equal(config$platform$code, platform_id)
  expect_true(config$platform$code %in% get_valid_platform_ids())
  
  return(TRUE)
}
```

## Subsection 2: Migration Timeline {#migration-timeline}

| Phase | Timeline | Action |
|-------|----------|--------|
| **Immediate** | Now | Create 26_platform_apis directory structure |
| **Week 1** | Days 1-7 | Migrate Cyberbiz API from archive |
| **Week 2** | Days 8-14 | Migrate eBay API and create common utilities |
| **Week 3** | Days 15-21 | Update all ETL scripts to use new API structure |
| **Week 4** | Days 22-28 | Add comprehensive tests and documentation |
| **Ongoing** | Continuous | Add new platforms following this architecture |

---

*Based on: Fundamental Law Article 94*  
*Related to: [MP092 Platform ID Standard](MP092_platform_id_standard.qmd), [MP065 Platform Configuration](MP065_platform_configuration_management.qmd)*  
*Implements: Unified platform API architecture for multi-source data integration*
